content.text: — enabling entire codebases (75,000+ lines), dozens of research papers, or complete API documentation in a single request. Available on

: Opus 4.6 scores 78.3% on MRCR v2 benchmark (highest among frontier models)
-
Traditional context limitations created fundamental problems
: Load 75k+ lines → 200K limit → agent fails at turn 30
: Process 100+ legal contracts → summarize → lose critical details
: Complete API docs + 100 tool calls → context exhausted → coherence lost
: Model forgets middle of input → severe degradation
For your Box Hill distributed systems work

: Load full codebase → understand cross-file dependencies
-

: Include complete docs + tool definitions → agent coheres
-

: Full conversation trace in context → no re-explaining
-
: "What was once impossible is now reality"
Best accuracy in first 30–40% of context

┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 1M tokens total capacity           │
│ ├─ Input: 1M tokens                │
│ ├─ Output: 4K tokens (max_tokens) │
│ └─ Media: 600 images/PDF pages    │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘

0–300K tokens:  ✅ Excellent recall (start = prime)
300K–700K tokens: ⚠️ Gradual degradation (middle = lost)
700K–1M tokens:  ⚠️ Significant degradation (end = recovery)

1M context → compaction still triggers at ~95%
Compaction = "washing machine"
  ├─ Takes entire context
  ├─ Spin-dries into summary
  └─ Result: 50K tokens but loses critical details
: Build agents with 100+ tool calls + complete API docs
: Start with 200K, move to 1M when ready
: 1M for current work + Memory Tool for cross-session persistence
: 1M for full trace + /memories for root cause

:
- Claude Platform (cloud)
- Microsoft Azure Foundry
- Google Vertex AI
- Claude Code (Max/Team/Enterprise plans)

# Example: Load full TypeScript monorepo
cat $(find src/ -name "*.ts") > codebase.txt
cat codebase.txt | wc -l
# → 75,342 lines
: Claude understands project architecture + cross-file dependencies.
: Analyze relationships across 100+ documents with full context.
: Agent coheres across hundreds of tool calls.

: "What was once impossible is now reality"
-
: Work on 75,000+ lines while maintaining high accuracy

: "1M token context has supercharged autonomous capabilities"
-
: Full agent trace in one context window
: ZDR eligible → data not stored after API response
: Cap at 600 images/PDF pages per request
For your Box Hill distributed systems work with monorepo refactoring and multi-file API analysis, I'd use
for focused edits. This gives you highest recall when you need it, speed when you don't.
